perturbation.original_text:
5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person:
a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or
b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or
c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include:
a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers;
b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5
c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.changed_text:
5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person:
a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or
b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or
c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include:
a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers;
b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5
c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction within Section 5. The original text states "This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations..." The changed text contradicts this by changing "not intended" to "intended" which means "This Agreement is intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations..." which contradicts the original clause about accepting employment with competing organizations, leading to confusion about the employee's rights. This introduces uncertainty as to whether the employee can work for a competitor, contradicting the original intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person:
a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or
b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or
c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include:
a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers;
b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5
c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 modifies "This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations..." to "This Agreement is intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations..." This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement as highlighted in the perturbation explanation, introducing a clear and significant inconsistency within Section 5 itself, also listed as the 'Contradicted Text'. Since the changed text directly clashes with what was originally stated about the employee's ability to work for a competitor, creating a strong inconsistency in the in-text obligation, it follows the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, therefore creating a strong, impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction will not begin until Employee is in full compliance with Section 5 and/or 6 .
perturbation.changed_text: Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction will begin immediately regardless of the compliance with Section 5 and/or 6 .
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 7 by changing when the post-termination restriction begins. Originally, it started only after full compliance with Sections 5 and/or 6; now it starts immediately, regardless of compliance. This contradicts the original clause providing a grace period for compliance, creating uncertainty about the enforcement timeline for the non-compete and non-solicitation clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Tolling of Covenants . Employee acknowledges that a Banking Organization is entitled to the full post-termination restrictions on the activities set forth in Sections 5 and 6 , as applicable. Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction will begin immediately regardless of the compliance with Section 5 and/or 6 .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 directly contradicts the original intention of allowing a grace period for compliance with Sections 5 and 6 before the post-termination restriction begins. The original text states the restriction won't begin until full compliance, while the changed text states it begins immediately regardless of compliance. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction remains. This creates a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of inconsistencies. As such, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will not suffer irreparable damage and injury and will have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 9. It directly contradicts the basis for injunctive relief. The original text asserts irreparable harm and lack of an adequate legal remedy, justifying injunctive relief. The altered text asserts the opposite: no irreparable harm and an adequate legal remedy. This undermines the enforceability of Sections 4, 5, and 6, as it removes the justification for injunctive relief, creating uncertainty about how breaches will be addressed. This directly contradicts the original intention of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Available Relief . Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will not suffer irreparable damage and injury and will have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, 5, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, even though they are located at the same part of the document. The original text states that a Banking Organization will suffer irreparable damage and does not have an adequate remedy at law. However, the changed text negates this, claiming that a Banking Organization will *not* suffer irreparable damage and *does* have an adequate remedy at law. The contradicted text then goes on to detail the available relief (injunctive relief) based on the premise that irreparable damage will occur. Since the changed text removes the premise of irreparable damage it removes the justification for the Available Relief, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be terminated, amended or modified without the prior written consent of the Bank’s President and Chief Executive Officer and Employee.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be terminated, amended or modified with the oral consent of any of the Bank’s Vice Presidents and Employee.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 13. The original text specifies that modifications require written consent from the Bank’s President and CEO and the Employee, while the altered text states that modifications can be made with oral consent from any of the Bank's Vice Presidents and the Employee. This contradicts the original requirement for written consent from specific individuals, making it unclear how the agreement can be legally modified and introducing uncertainty about the validity of any amendments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Entire Agreement/Modification/Waiver . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement among the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and expressly supersedes any prior agreements between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall be terminated, amended or modified with the oral consent of any of the Bank’s Vice Presidents and Employee. No failure or delay by a Banking Organization in exercising any right or remedy under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any waiver by a Banking Organization under this Agreement operate or be construed as a continuing waiver or a waiver of any subsequent breach or noncompliance hereunder. No single or partial exercise of any right or remedy by a Banking Organization shall preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right or remedy. Any waiver by a Banking Organization under this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by the Bank’s President and Chief Executive Officer. A waiver shall operate only as to the specific term or condition 7 waived and will not constitute a waiver for the future or act on anything other than that which is specifically waived.
perturbation.justification: The original text required written consent from the Bank's President and CEO and the Employee for any termination, amendment, or modification. The changed text allows for oral consent from any of the Bank's Vice Presidents and the Employee for the same actions. The contradicted text includes the changed text. This directly contradicts the original requirement for written consent from specific individuals. The change makes it unclear how the agreement can be legally modified, creating uncertainty about the validity of any amendments, and thus constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, aligning with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
